fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Alabama Audiologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Alabama - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: audiologist license reciprocity alabama, how to transfer audiologist license to alabama, audiologist compact alabama, alabama audiologist endorsement application

board.name: Alabama Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
board.mailing_address: 201 S. Union Street, Suite 444, Montgomery, AL 36104
board.physical_address: 100 N. Union St., Suite 724, Montgomery, AL 36130

quick_facts.exam_name: Audiology Examination of the Praxis Series administered by the Educational Testing Service

license_types.title: AuD, CCC-A
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a audiologist in Alabama.

steps.title: Obtain a Qualifying Degree
steps.description: Earn a Master's or Doctoral degree in Audiology from an accredited educational institution.

steps.title: Pass the National Examination
steps.description: Achieve a passing score on the Praxis II examination in Audiology (ETS Exam #5342).

steps.title: Create Account and Complete Application
steps.description: Access the ABESPA Online Services Portal, create a new user account, and begin the initial Audiologist license application.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documents
steps.description: Submit required documentation, such as official transcripts and national examination scores, either by uploading directly to the ABESPA Online Services Portal or arranging for official submission to ABESPA.

steps.title: Pay Application and License Fees
steps.description: Pay the initial application fee ($75) and the license fee ($100) through the ABESPA Online Services Portal.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the Praxis. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Alabama audiologist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Alabama have reciprocity for audiologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Alabama participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Alabama without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to Alabama?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Alabama while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Audiologist in Alabama
hero.dek: To apply for an Audiologist license in Alabama, applicants must use the ABESPA Online Services Portal.

official_links.registration_steps: Applicants must create an online account using a valid email address on the ABESPA Online Services Portal and then proceed to complete the initial application.
